Mary L. Leach
527 South L street 
[.,,, I

Lompoc, cA 93436 
lr;" '/
February 2a, L99o
Kathryn E. Donovan, General council
Fair Politica] Practice Commission
428 J street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 9 581- 4
RE: Request for confirrnation of telephone advice
Dear !Is. Donovan:
This is a request for confirmation of tel-ephone advice I received
from !1s. Blanca Breeze at the FPPC on February 27, L99O. At that
time I explained to l,ls. Breeze that I was recently appointed a
Planning Comrnissioner for the City of Lompoc. Prior to ny ap-
pointnent, as a citizen of Lompoc, I spoke out against a re-zone
apptication by former Planning Commission chairman, Mr. I,Iichae1
KnoeII. I spoke out against this re-zone at a public hearing
before the city Council and in the local newspaper. Although I
did turn in a petition with approximately 30 signatures on it, at
the Council I stated for the record I was speaking for myself and
my husband.
I was not compensated in any way for my voice. I have a small
collection consultant business but none of the opponents s/ho have
spoken out against this re-zone are cfients of mine. At least
952 of ny clients live out of town.
On December 5, 1989 the City Council of Lonpoc approved a re-zone
for l,Iichael Knoell at 4l-0 south H Street in Lompoc. The
devel-oper had requested his l-0,500 sq. ft. Iot in the middle of
the block in an R-2 zone be re-zoned PD (PLanned Development ) so
that he might divide the lot into three tots to be sold independ-
ently. 
on or about December 77, 1989 the City suspended the
developer's permit on a third building proposed for the lot,
which was atready developed with two homes. on January 2, L99o
the opponents of the re-zone, myself included, gave testirnony to
the cj.ty council in the be1ief that the staff had misled the
council, advising that such a re-zone approval would set a pre-
cedence not in the best interest of the neighborhood or the city
as a whole, and suggesting that a few ordinances had been mis-
interpreted in the developer's favor.
The city Council initiated a moratorium on all R-2 building,
sending the re-zone back to the Planning Cornmission and
requesting that the ordinances be reviewed and clarified so that
each ordinance would be applied equally to all developers.
(Page 1 of 3)


A fe!, weeks later I was appointed to the
That same night the counci.l anended the
development.
Planning Commission.
moratoriurn to include R-3
on February 13, 1990 the Planning commission was scheduled to
have a discussion regarding the issues related to the R-2, R-3
rnoratorium. on the afternoon of Feb. 13 the city Attorney asked
to speak with me, stated he had received an inquiry about my bias
in this case and suggested I might have a conftict of interest.
He briefly mentioned a monetary clause but said first he must
have staff check the radius of my property from the proposed lot
re-zone. He also asked me if I owned R-2 property stating that
\,roufd definitely be a conflict of interest. 
I do not.
It{y home is within a 2500 ft. radius of the proposed re-zone but
it is in an R-l--l-o zone. This re-zone would not impact my neigh-
borhood or affect its value any nore than j.t would impact or
affect the value of any home in the city.
I explained to l{s. Breeze that I felt singled out. I could not
understand how my conftict \^ras any greater than another
commissioner r.rho had previously voted on this issue and also
lived within 25oo ft. of the property, or a third who had voted
before and o\4/ned R-2 and R-3 property in the city. 
I have
nothing to gain rnonetarily from my opposition.
on the evening of Feb. 13 the Staff and Pfanning Comrnission chose
to change the ordinances in the developer's favor rather than
clarify thern since they believed it was not important what they
s/ere but what they should be. I objected to this action. I
believed the Commj.ssion had strayed from the Council's objective
to clarify the laws as they exj.sted.
on February 27, 1,990 the text amendment recommendation to the
council cane up for vote agiain. Since the test amendments
involved the very issues which were raised at the Knoell re-zone
the city Attorney felt that bias could be inplied and suggested I
not vote even though this decision was legislative not quasi-
judicial and $rou1d affect me and the public equalfy as much as it
affected the developer.
In addition, on February 27tr, the re-zone application came before
the commission again as a totally separate agenda item. The city
Attorney fe1t that since I was 'rembroiled in the controversy"
that bias could be implied and therefore I should not vote on the
re-zone to avoid the rrappearance of impropriety. rt He also
recommended I contact the FPPC.
!Is. Breeze explaj.ned to me that I have no conflict of interest
since I have no monetary stake; that my First Amendnent rights
give me the right to speak and that I was appointed specifically
for ny voj-ce. In addition, she explained that everyone cones to
a public seat with some bias and therefore my bias $ras no greater
than any other.
(Page 2 of 3)


Please send me lrritten confirnation of the telephone advice given
by Blanca Breeze on February 27, !99O. If you have any question
regarding the above information don't hesitate to caII me at
(805) 736-3954. Thank you for your time and attention to this
rnatter .
Respectfully Yours,
m*9/"6.u,-,
ltary fleach -
Planning Comrnissioner
City of Lonpoc, california
(Page 3 of 3)

sections.question_synthetic: Does a newly appointed Planning Commissioner have a conflict of interest under FPPC rules if she previously spoke as a citizen against a re-zone application and now must vote on related matters, given that she has no financial stake and lives in a different zone?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a member of the public asking for written confirmation of prior telephone advice. The document lacks formal Q&A sections but contains substantial factual narrative about the applicant's situation. No FPPC response/conclusion is present in this document. The telephone advice is summarized at the end of page 2, where Ms. Breeze allegedly advised no conflict exists. Date discrepancy noted: letter dated February 28, 1990 but references telephone call on February 27, 1990.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Does a newly appointed Planning Commissioner have a conflict of interest under FPPC rules if she previously spoke as a citizen against a re-zone application and now must vote on related matters, given that she has no financial stake and lives in a different zone?

embedding.summary: Mary Leach, a newly appointed Planning Commissioner for Lompoc, requests written confirmation of telephone advice from FPPC staff (Ms. Blanca Breeze) regarding whether she has a conflict of interest in voting on matters related to a re-zone application she previously opposed as a citizen.
